summary: The gateway terminal surface was located: the public GUI is served from Cloudflare Pages at https://mestumre.dev, while API calls route to https://gateway.mestumre.dev through the GKE gateway and Cloudflare tunnel. The existing bump mechanism is the ops playbook automation/cloudflare/gke_gateway_edge.yaml action=pages/deploy, which builds repos/gui and runs wrangler pages deploy to Cloudflare Pages project noetl-gui. Execution stopped before deploy because this environment has no CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and wrangler cannot operate non-interactively without it.

phases.name: identify_the_bump_mechanism
phases.case: Case C - manual Cloudflare Pages deploy via ops playbook
phases.mechanism: Run the local ops playbook with action=pages or action=deploy after exporting CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN. The playbook runs npm ci, builds repos/gui with VITE_API_MODE=gateway and VITE_GATEWAY_URL=https://gateway.mestumre.dev, then executes npx --yes wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name noetl-gui --branch main.
phases.candidate_command: cd repos/ops && CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<token> noetl run automation/cloudflare/gke_gateway_edge.yaml --runtime local --set action=pages --set gui_repo_dir=../gui --set pages_project_name=noetl-gui --set pages_branch=main --set gui_domain=mestumre.dev --set gateway_public_url=https://gateway.mestumre.dev

version_context.note: v1.11.0 is the semantic-release version commit for the app:form {fieldId} feature.

phases.name: execute_the_bump
phases.blocker: CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN is not present in the environment. npx --yes wrangler pages project list fails with: In a non-interactive environment, it is necessary to set a CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN environment variable.
phases.action_taken: No Cloudflare project, DNS, tunnel, or Pages deployment was modified.

phases.name: smoke_the_gateway_terminal
phases.reason: Stopped at the Cloudflare-token blocker before deployment. Public GUI opens to the Auth0/token login page in this browser session, so the terminal sequence was not runnable without credentials/session.

findings.finding: The GUI surface is Cloudflare Pages, not an in-cluster Kubernetes gui Deployment.
findings.evidence: https://mestumre.dev returns static Vite assets with Cloudflare headers. The ops playbook names Cloudflare Pages project noetl-gui and custom domain mestumre.dev.

findings.finding: The API/gateway surface is gateway.mestumre.dev through the GKE gateway and Cloudflare tunnel.
findings.evidence: ops playbook maps gateway.mestumre.dev to gateway.gateway.svc.cluster.local:80, and GKE has cloudflare/noetl-gke-gateway-tunnel running 2/2 plus gateway/gateway on ghcr.io/noetl/gateway:v2.10.0.

findings.finding: The currently served public bundle is older than the travel-command bundle.
findings.evidence: The live bundle includes the /travel route and app:form code but does not contain literal travel command strings such as 'started travel agent', 'usage: travel', or 'automation/agents/travel/runtime'.

findings.finding: Cloudflare deploy is blocked on credentials owned by Kadyapam.
findings.evidence: No CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN/CF token is exported, and wrangler reports that a CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN is required in a non-interactive environment.

next_action_for_kadyapam: Export a scoped CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN locally and rerun the Pages deploy path, or provide a one-shot shell with that token available. The token needs Cloudflare Pages Edit for project noetl-gui and the zone/tunnel permissions already documented by automation/cloudflare/gke_gateway_edge.yaml.
secrets_hygiene: No Cloudflare token, Auth0 token, or session token was read, printed, or stored.
